steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with a dangling-modifier trap: "Working with equipment designed for a billionth-of-a-meter scale, ______". The participial phrase describes who is doing the working, so the subject of the main clause must be the people using that equipment — the nanoengineers, not the nanoparticles.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — synthesized selenium nanoparticles used to target harmful cells in the body without damaging other cells have been created by nanoengineers.: Subject is "nanoparticles" — nanoparticles cannot work with equipment; dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — nanoengineers have synthesized selenium nanoparticles that are used to target harmful cells in the body without damaging other cells.: Subject is "nanoengineers" — the people who logically work with nanoscale equipment. Modifier attaches correctly. ✓
  • Choice C — nanoengineers' synthesized selenium nanoparticles are used to target harmful cells in the body without damaging other cells.: The possessive "nanoengineers'" makes the nanoparticles the grammatical subject, not the nanoengineers themselves; the modifier dangles. ✗
  • Choice D — selenium nanoparticles that have been synthesized by nanoengineers are used to target harmful cells in the body without damaging other cells.: Subject is "nanoparticles" again; nanoparticles cannot use equipment. ✗
That is the correct answer.
